Cisco designs and sells a broad range of technologies that power the Internet. We are integrating our product portfolios across networking, security, collaboration, applications and the cloud to create highly secure, intelligent platforms for our customers' digital businesses. These platforms are designed to help our customers manage more users, devices and things connecting to their networks. This will enable us to provide customers with a highly secure, intelligent platform for their digital business.
We conduct our business globally and manage our business by geography. Our business is organized into the following three geographic segments: Americas; Europe, Middle East, and Africa (EMEA); and Asia Pacific, Japan, and China (APJC).
Our products and technologies are grouped into the following categories: Secure, Agile Networks; Internet for the Future; Collaboration; End-to-End Security; Optimized Application Experiences; and Other Products. In addition to our product offerings, we provide a broad range of service offerings, including technical support services and advanced services. Increasingly, we are delivering our technologies through software and services. Our customers include businesses of all sizes, public institutions, governments, and service providers, including large webscale providers. These customers often look to us as a strategic partner to help them use information technology (IT) to differentiate themselves and drive positive business outcomes.
We were incorporated in California in 1984 and reincorporated in Delaware in 2021. Our headquarters are in San Jose, California. The mailing address of our headquarters is 170 West Tasman Drive, San Jose, California 95134-1706, and our telephone number at that location is (408) 526-4000. Our website is www.cisco.com. Through a link on the Investor Relations section of our website, we make available the following filings as soon as reasonably practicable after they are electronically filed with or furnished to the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) at sec.gov: our Annual Report on Form 10-K, Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q, Current Reports on Form 8-K, and any amendments to those reports filed or furnished pursuant to Section 13(a) or 15(d) of the Exchange Act. All such filings are available free of charge. The
…[truncated 19676 chars]…
ross our security portfolio focused on cloud-based offerings, AI-driven threat detection and end-to-end security architectures. Additionally, we continue to invest in expanding our SASE architecture by delivering combined network and security functionality in a single cloud-native service.
5
Table of Contents
Optimized Application Experiences
The Optimized Application Experiences product category consists of our full stack observability and network assurance offerings. Our full stack observability offerings are designed to bring together and provide end-to-end visibility of our customer's environments across applications, networks, multi-cloud infrastructures and the Internet, to help deliver full stack observability for modern environments and drive relevant real-time insights. Our Intersight Infrastructure Services, a cloud-delivered SaaS offering, provides lifecycle operations management, automation, orchestration, and monitoring capabilities for customers' infrastructure deployments from Data Center to the Edge. Our monitoring and analytics offering, AppDynamics, monitors performance across different application-related domains. Our network assurance offering, ThousandEyes, is a network intelligence platform that provides in-depth visibility into network and Internet performance. It enables organizations to see, understand, and improve every digital experience and assure seamless connectivity for their modern digital environments.
Services
In addition to our product offerings, we provide a broad range of service and support options for our customers. Our overall service and support offerings are combined into one organization, Customer Experience, that is responsible for the end-to-end customer experience.
Our support and maintenance services help our customers ensure their products operate efficiently, remain available, and benefit from the most up-to-date system and application software. These services help customers protect their network investments, manage risk, and minimize downtime for systems running mission-critical applications.
We also provide comprehensive advisory services that are focused on responsive, preventive, and consultative support of our technologies for specific networking needs. We are investing in and expanding advisory services in the areas of software, cloud, security, and analytics, which reflects our strategy of selling customer outcomes. We are focused on three priorities: utilizing technology advisory services to drive higher product and services; assessment and migration services providing the tools, expertise and methodologies to enable our customers to migrate to new technology platforms; and providing optimization services aligned with customers' business expectations.
Customers and Markets
Many factors influence the IT, collaboration, and networking requirements of our customers. These include the size of the organization, number and types of technology systems, geographic location, and business applications deployed throughout the customer's network. Our customer base is not limited to any specific industry, geography, or market segment. Our customers primarily operate in the following markets: enterprise, commercial, service provider, and public sector.
Enterprise
Enterprise businesses are large regional, national, or global organizations with multiple locations or branch offices. Many enterprise businesses have unique IT, collaboration, and networking needs within a multivendor environment. We offer service and support packages, financing, and managed network services, primarily through our service provider partners. We sell these products through a network of third-party application and technology vendors and channel partners, as well as selling directly to these customers.
Commercial
The commercial market represents larger, or midmarket and small businesses. We sell to our midmarket customers through a combination of our direct sales force and channel partners. These customers typically require the latest advanced technologies that our enterprise customers demand, but with less complexity. Small businesses require information technologies and communications products that are easy to configure, install, and maintain. We sell to these smaller organizations within the commercial market primarily through channel partners.
Service Providers
Service providers offer data, voice, video, and mobile/wireless services to businesses, governments, utilities, and consumers worldwide. The service provider market includes regional, national, and international wireline carriers, webscale operators as well as Internet, cable, and wireless providers. We also include media, broadcast, and content providers within our service provider market, as the lines in the telecommunications industry continue to blur between traditional network-based, content-based and application-based services. Service providers use a variety of our products and services for their own networks. In addition, many service providers use Cisco data center, virtualization, and collaboration technologies to offer managed or Internet-based services to their business customers. Compared with other customers, service providers are more likely to require network design, deployment, and support services because of the greater scale and higher complexity of their networks, whose requirements are addressed, we believe, by our architectural approach.
6
Table of Contents
Public Sector
The public sector market includes federal, state and local governments, as well as educational institution customers. Many public sector customers have unique IT, collaboration, and networking needs within a multi-vendor environment. We sell to public sector customers through a network of third-party application and technology vendors, channel partners, as well as through direct sales.
Sales Overview
As of the end of fiscal 2023, our worldwide sales and marketing functions consisted of approximately 26,000 employees, including managers, sales representatives, and technical support personnel. We sell our products and services both directly and indirectly through a variety of channels with support from our salesforce. A substantial portion of our products and services is sold indirectly through channel partners, and the remainder is sold through direct sales. Channel partners include systems integrators, service providers, other third-party resellers, and distributors.
Systems integrators and service providers typically sell directly to end users and often provide system installation, technical support, professional services, and other support services in addition to network equipment sales. Systems integrators also typically integrate our products into an overall solution. Some service providers are also systems integrators.
Distributors may hold inventory and sell to systems integrators, service providers, and other third-party resellers. We refer to sales through distributors as our two-tier system of sales to the end customer. Revenue from two-tier distributors is recognized based on a sell-in method. These distributors may be given business terms that allow them to return a limited portion of inventory, receive credits for changes in selling prices, receive certain rebates, and participate in various cooperative marketing programs.

**2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**  
- Earnings Quality: EPS shows consistent growth with TTM net income of $12.61B. Quarterly EPS growth is positive, and YoY EPS growth of +41.2% exceeds revenue growth, indicating operating leverage. No cash-flow data to assess cash conversion.  
- Balance-Sheet Resilience: Debt-to-equity ratio is 0.189, with total debt of $8.40B versus equity of $44.35B. Equity has grown steadily, and debt has declined, supporting resilience.

**3. Valuation Versus Supplied History**

- Latest p_b is 4.976, within the range of 4.1 to 5.6. Earnings yield is 0.057 (5.7%).  
- Valuation is mid-range relative to its recent history, not indicating extreme cheapness or expensiveness.

**4. Price Level and Return Context Versus Fundamentals**  
- Price: Close on 2023-09-29 is $53.76, with a 52-week high of $58.19 and low of $38.60.  
- Returns: Quarterly return (from 2023-06-30) is +3.9%; annual return (from 2022-09-28) is +30.0%.  
- Fundamental Path: Annual revenue growth is +16.0% and EPS growth is +41.2%. Price appreciation of +30.0% aligns with fundamental growth, but EPS growth is stronger, suggesting valuation compression or market discounting future growth.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**  
- Upside: Strong YoY earnings growth of +41.2%, improving margins (net margin 0.221), and a resilient balance sheet with low debt (debt-to-equity 0.189). If growth persists, valuation could expand from current mid-range levels.  
- Downside: Absence of cash-flow data limits assessment of cash generation. Recent price momentum (+30.0% annually) may lead to pullbacks if growth decelerates, and PE could compress from 17.511 toward the low end of 13.9.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**  
Based on supplied numbers, CSCO exhibits solid revenue and earnings growth, a healthy balance sheet, and valuation within historical bounds. The risk-reward is balanced: upside from continued growth and potential valuation expansion, downside from growth slowdown and valuation compression, with cash-flow visibility absent.
CSCO.qualitative_report:
# CSCO — Qualitative Report (Decision Date: 2023-10-01)

---

## 1. Business / Franchise Read

Cisco designs and sells a broad range of technologies powering the Internet, organized across five product categories: **Secure Agile Networks** (switching, routing, wireless, compute), **Internet for the Future** (routed optical networking, Silicon One, 5G), **Collaboration** (Webex suite, CPaaS), **End-to-End Security** (SASE, XDR, cloud/application security), and **Optimized Application Experiences** (full-stack observability via AppDynamics, ThousandEyes, Intersight). Geographically segmented into Americas, EMEA, and APJC (10-K, 2023-09-07).

The company is pursuing a **software-and-subscription transformation**, shifting historically hardware-centric networking revenue toward recurring software, SaaS, and as-a-service consumption (Cisco Plus). Strategy pillars include AI/ML integration across portfolios, hybrid work enablement, multicloud architectures, and sustainability. Customers span enterprise, commercial, service provider (including webscale), and public sector, sold both directly and through a two-tier channel of distributors, integrators, and resellers (10-K, 2023-09-07).

Cisco holds a dominant installed base in enterprise networking and is leveraging that position to cross-sell security, observability, and collaboration software — a credible "platform" story, though execution on the software mix shift remains the central franchise question.

---

## 2. Recent Developments (Dated)

| Date | Development | Source |
|---|---|---|
| **2023-09-29** | Cisco Canada partners with Ontario government and TakingITGlobal on digital equity for remote Indigenous communities — incremental ESG/reputational positioning. | Company news |
| **2023-09-25** | Reported that Cisco announced agreement to acquire **Splunk** (~$28B implied from context) to bolster observability/security and cement software-centric transformation. | Company news |
| **2023-09-25** | Article noting CSCO stock "appears to be turning a corner" but still well below its 2000-era record high. | Company news |
| **2023-09-28** | CSCO closed at $53.88, up 1.28% on the day; described as a "trending stock" on Zacks. | Company news |
| **2023-08-17** | Q4 FY2023 earnings: **Secure Agile Networks segment revenue up 33% YoY and 8% sequentially**, driven by backlog digestion. Morningstar characterized demand as "coming down to earth but still healthy." | Web context (Morningstar) |
| **2023-08-17** | **Q1 FY2024 revenue guidance of $14.5B–$14.7B**, vs. $13.6B in Q1 FY2023 — implies ~7–8% YoY growth. | Web context (Nasdaq) |
| **2023-09-08** | Stock returned ~33% over the prior year, driven by revenue/profit growth and expanding valuation multiples. | Web context (Motley Fool) |
| **2023-09-10** | Article identifies AI infrastructure and two near-term non-AI catalysts as potential growth drivers, though AI's contribution is characterized as moderate on current timelines. | Web context (InvestorPlace) |
| **2023-09-29** | >238,000 global tech workers laid off in 2023 across >1,000 companies — relevant demand backdrop for enterprise IT spending. | Company news |

---

## 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets

**Risks:**

- **Caste-discrimination lawsuit (2023-08-17):** A Dalit Indian engineer alleges harassment, discrimination, and retaliation by Cisco, with the Ambedkar International Centre filing supporting action. Potential reputational and legal exposure, particularly in talent-sensitive markets. *(Confirmed from text)*

- **Demand normalization:** Morningstar (2023-08-17) noted demand is "coming down to earth" after a period of backlog-fueled growth. The 33% YoY networking segment growth was partly driven by working through accumulated order backlog, which is inherently non-recurring. As backlog normalizes, organic growth rate likely decelerates. *(Inference supported by text)*

- **Macro/IT spending headwinds:** The 2023 tech-layoff wave (238,000+ workers, 2023-09-29) signals broader enterprise cost discipline that could moderate networking and collaboration spend. *(Confirmed from text; causal link to CSCO is inference)*

- **Splunk acquisition execution risk:** The announced acquisition is transformative in scale and integrates a large software/data platform. Integration complexity, cultural fit, and potential dilution or distraction are inherent risks, though not explicitly detailed in the supplied text. *(Partially confirmed — acquisition reported 2023-09-25; execution risk is inference)*

- **Prolonged valuation discount:** Stock noted as still below its 2000 record high (2023-09-25), suggesting structural skepticism about Cisco's ability to re-rate as a growth compounder versus a mature cash-generating franchise. *(Confirmed from text)*

**Offsets:**

- **Software/subscription transition progress:** 10-K (2023-09-07) emphasizes increasing software and subscription mix across networking, security, and collaboration. Splunk (2023-09-25) accelerates this trajectory and adds a large recurring-revenue observability/data platform.

- **Installed base and cross-sell:** Dominant enterprise networking position creates a captive audience for security, observability, and collaboration upsell — a credible flywheel described in the 10-K strategic pillars.

- **AI infrastructure optionality:** Cisco Silicon One and AI-scale infrastructure are positioned as enabling technologies for AI workloads, offering a medium-term catalyst even if near-term AI revenue contribution is modest (2023-09-10).

- **Solid Q1 FY2024 guidance:** $14.5B–$14.7B guidance implies continued healthy growth, and Morningstar described fundamentals as "still healthy" (2023-08-17).

- **Shareholder returns:** ~33% total return over the trailing year (2023-09-08), supported by both earnings growth and multiple expansion.

---

## 4. Qualitative Assessment

**Assessment: Cautiously Positive / Neutral-to-Positive**

Cisco's franchise is well-established with a dominant networking installed base and a credible multi-year software/SaaS transition underway. The Splunk acquisition (2023-09-25) is a bold strategic step that, if executed, could meaningfully accelerate recurring revenue and expand Cisco's addressable market in observability and data. Q1 FY2024 guidance (2023-08-17) and robust recent networking segment growth (33% YoY) demonstrate continued momentum.

However, key uncertainties temper enthusiasm: (a) the organic growth rate is likely decelerating as backlog normalizes; (b) the Splunk integration is large and unproven; (c) macro softness in enterprise IT spending is a real headwind; and (d) the stock's persistent valuation discount relative to its history suggests the market remains skeptical of a fundamental re-rating. The qualitative picture is one of a solid, improving franchise with meaningful catalysts but also execution and macro dependencies that warrant balanced positioning.

The transformative Splunk acquisition adds strategic optionality but also execution risk, resulting in a balanced risk-reward profile.

The transformative Splunk acquisition adds strategic optionality but also execution risk, resulting in a balanced risk-reward profile.

Item 1. Business
Overview
Walgreens Boots Alliance, Inc., a Delaware corporation ("Walgreens Boots Alliance" or the "Company"), is an integrated healthcare, pharmacy and retail leader serving millions of customers and patients every day, with a 170-year heritage of caring for communities. The Company is proud of its contributions to healthy communities, a healthy planet, an inclusive workplace and a sustainable marketplace. Walgreens Boots Alliance is a participant of the United Nations Global Compact and adheres to its principles-based approach to responsible business. The Company has been recognized for its commitment to operating sustainably; it is an index component of the Dow Jones Sustainability Indices ("DJSI") and was named to the 100 Best Corporate Citizens of 2022.
A trusted, global innovator in retail pharmacy with approximately 13,000 locations across the U.S., Europe and Latin America, Walgreens Boots Alliance plays a critical role in the healthcare ecosystem. The Company is reimagining local healthcare and well-being for all as part of its purpose – to create more joyful lives through better health. Through dispensing medicines, improving access to a wide range of health services, providing high quality health and beauty products and offering anytime, anywhere convenience acro
…[truncated 20706 chars]…
nts of the segment's sales are Pharmacy (the sale of prescription drugs and provision of pharmacy-related services) and Retail (the sale of healthcare and retail products including non-prescription drugs, health and wellness, beauty and personal care, and consumables and general merchandise). The segment's sales are subject to the influence of seasonality, particularly the cough, cold and flu seasons and winter holiday. This seasonality also can affect the segment's proportion of sales between Retail and Pharmacy during certain periods. The components of the segment's fiscal year sales were as follows:
|  | Fiscal 2022 | Fiscal 2022 | Fiscal 2021 | Fiscal 2021 | Fiscal 2020 | Fiscal 2020 |
| Pharmacy | 74 | 74% | 76 | 76% | 75 | 75% |
| Retail | 26 | 26% | 24 | 24% | 25 | 25% |
| Total | 100 | 100% | 100 | 100% | 100 | 100% |
The Company filled 819.6 million prescriptions (including vaccinations) in the segment in fiscal 2022. Adjusted to 30-day equivalents, prescriptions filled were 1.2 billion in fiscal 2022. The Company fills prescriptions under Medicare, Medicaid and other publicly financed or sponsored health benefit and prescription drug plans and programs, including the federal 340B drug pricing program. Sales where reimbursement is received from managed care organizations, governmental agencies, PBM companies and private insurance were approximately 97% of the segment's fiscal 2022 Pharmacy sales.
The Company fills prescriptions for many state Medicaid public assistance programs. Sales from all such Medicaid plans were approximately 5% of the segment's fiscal 2022 sales. Sales from Medicare Part D plans were approximately 22% of the segment's fiscal 2022 sales.
The Company's myWalgreens Credit Card program features the myWalgreens Mastercard and the myWalgreens Credit Card. These cards are the first ever of their kind to reward more personalized wellbeing choices and offer industry-leading rewards at Walgreens locations, Walgreens.com, Duane Reade stores, via the Walgreens mobile app, and wherever Mastercard is accepted.
AmerisourceBergen supplies and distributes a significant amount of generic and branded pharmaceutical products to the segment's pharmacies. The Company purchases its non-pharmaceutical merchandise from numerous manufacturers and wholesalers.
The segment's sales, gross profit margin and gross profit are impacted by, among other things, both the percentage of prescriptions filled that are generic and the rate at which new generic drugs are introduced to the market. Because any number of factors outside of the Company's control can affect timing for a generic conversion, the Company faces substantial uncertainty in predicting when such conversions will occur and what effect they will have on particular future periods.
WBA Fiscal 2022 Form 10-K 5
Table of Contents
The current environment of the Company's pharmacy business also includes ongoing reimbursement pressure, a shift in pharmacy mix towards 90-day at retail (one prescription that is the equivalent of three 30-day prescriptions), an increased volume of Medicare Part D prescriptions and increased consumer use of prescription discount cards. Further consolidation among generic manufacturers coupled with changes in the number of major brand name drugs anticipated to undergo a conversion from branded to generic status may also result in gross margin pressures within the industry.
The Company continuously faces reimbursement pressure from PBM companies, government, health maintenance organizations, managed care organizations and other commercial third-party payers. Agreements with these payers are regularly subject to expiration, termination or renegotiation. In addition, plan changes with rate adjustments often occur in January and the Company's reimbursement arrangements may provide for rate adjustments at prescribed intervals during their term. The Company experienced lower reimbursement rates in fiscal 2022 as compared to the same period in the prior year. The Company expects these pressures to continue.
The Company has also worked to develop and expand its relationships with commercial third-party payers to enable new and/or improved market access via participation in pharmacy provider networks they offer. The prescription volume impact of new agreements and relationships typically is incremental over time.
The Company's 90-day at retail prescription drug offering is typically at a lower margin than comparable 30-day prescriptions, but provides the Company with the opportunity to increase business with patients with chronic prescription needs while offering increased convenience, helping facilitate improved prescription adherence and resulting in a lower cost to fill the 90-day prescription. Similarly, the specialty prescription business, which generates higher sales dollars per prescription, may result in gross margin pressures within the industry, as compared to generic prescription drugs. The segment's performance is also impacted by the current environment, including the uncertainty as a result of COVID-19. For more information, see Risk factors in Item 1A.
International
The International segment consists of pharmacy-led health and beauty retail businesses outside the U.S. and the pharmaceutical wholesaling and distribution business in Germany.
Pharmacy-led health and beauty retail businesses include Boots branded stores in the United Kingdom ("UK"), the Republic of Ireland and Thailand, the Benavides brand in Mexico and the Ahumada brand in Chile. Sales for these businesses are principally derived from the sale of prescription drugs and health and wellness, beauty, personal care and other consumer products. The Company operated 3,989 retail stores in the segment as of August 31, 2022 (see properties in Part I, Item 2 for information regarding geographic coverage) and has grown its omni-channel platform, including its online presence, in recent years. In the UK, the Company is a market leader and its retail stores are conveniently located with pharmacists well placed to provide a significant role in the provision of healthcare services, working closely with other primary healthcare providers in the communities the Company serves.
The Boots omni-channel offering is differentiated from that of competitors due to the product brands the Company owns, such as No7, Liz Earle, Soap & Glory, Botanics, Sleek MakeUp, Boots Pharmaceuticals and 'only at Boots' exclusive products, together with its long established reputation for trust and customer care. The Company's brands portfolio is enhanced by its in-house product research and development capabilities. The Company has introduced new beauty brands and beauty halls in key locations. Certain of the product brands of the Company are also sold by third-party retailers.
The Company's retail store networks are typically complemented by online platforms. In the UK, through the boots.com website and integrated mobile application, the 'order and collect' service normally allows customers to order from a range of over 37,000 products by 5:00 p.m. and collect the following day from approximately 98% of the UK's retail stores.
The Boots Advantage Card loyalty program, where customers earn points on purchases for redemption at a later date, continues to be a key element of the Boots offering. As of August 31, 2022, the number of active Boots Advantage Card members (members who have used their card in the last six months) totaled approximately 13 million.
In addition, Boots in the UK is one of the leaders in the optical market with 543 practices, of which 160 operated on a franchise basis as of August 31, 2022. Approximately 30% of these optical practices are located in Boots stores with the balance being standalone optical practices.

## WBA Quantitative Report — Decision Date 2023-10-01

### 1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow & Balance-Sheet Trajectory

**Revenue:** Quarterly revenue has trended upward over eight quarters, from $32.45B (Q4 Aug-2022) to $35.41B (Q3 May-2023). YoY growth in the latest quarter is +8.6% ($35.41B vs $32.60B). QoQ growth was a modest +1.6% ($35.41B vs $34.86B). TTM revenue is $136.11B.

**Earnings:** The trajectory is erratic and deeply negative on a TTM basis. TTM net income is **−$3.73B**; TTM operating income is **−$6.85B**. A single quarter (Q1 Nov-2022) produced EPS of **−$4.31** and operating income of **−$6.15B**, overwhelming all other quarters. Stripping that out, Q3 May-2023 EPS of **$0.14** is still the weakest "clean" quarter versus Q2's **$0.81**, Q3-2022's **$0.33**, and Q2-2022's **$1.02**. YoY EPS declined **−57.6%** ($0.14 vs $0.33). QoQ EPS collapsed **−82.7%** ($0.14 vs $0.81).

**Margins:** Latest-quarter gross margin = $6.59B / $35.41B = **18.6%**, broadly in line with the trailing range (~18.6–22.8%). Operating margin turned negative at **−1.3%** (−$477M / $35.41B). Net margin is reported at **−2.8%** (per valuation panel), with Q3 net loss of **−$48M**. The prior quarter showed a positive operating margin of +0.6% ($197M).

**Cash flow:** *No cash-flow data available in the supplied evidence.* Free cash flow is marked **n/a** across all periods.

**Balance sheet:** Total assets **$98.61B**, total liabilities **$69.09B**, total equity **$29.36B** (May-2023). Equity has ranged from **$24.58B** (Nov-2022, impaired quarter) to **$30.87B** (Feb-2022). Latest available total debt is **$11.71B** (Aug-2022), implying a debt-to-equity ratio of ~0.40×. Liabilities-to-assets stands at **70.1%**. Book value per share (implied from P/B = 0.654 and price $22.24) ≈ **$34.00**.

### 2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience

**Earnings quality — weak and deteriorating:**
- The TTM picture is dominated by a **−$3.82B** net-loss quarter (Nov-2022), likely a large non-cash charge or impairment given the operating loss of **−$6.15B**. This makes current trailing earnings essentially unrepresentative of normalized power.
- Stripping that quarter, the most recent three "clean" quarters still show declining profitability: EPS **1.02 → 0.33 → 0.81 → 0.14**. The latest quarter's operating loss of **−$477M** versus prior-quarter operating income of **$197M** signals deterioration, not normalization.
- Gross profit declined QoQ by **−6.6%** ($6.59B vs $7.05B) while revenue grew, indicating cost pressure.
- **Cash-flow data is entirely unavailable**, so accrual quality cannot be independently assessed.

**Balance-sheet resilience — adequate but eroding:**
- Equity of **$29.36B** provides a meaningful buffer. The charge quarter (Nov-2022) temporarily reduced equity to **$24.58B**, a **−19.7%** decline from the prior quarter's **$30.58B**, demonstrating vulnerability to write-downs.
- Leverage (debt/equity ~0.40×) is moderate, but without cash-flow data, debt-servicing capacity is unverifiable.

### 3. Valuation Versus Supplied History

| Metric | Latest (Sep-2023) | 12-Month Range | Direction |
|--------|-------------------|----------------|-----------|
| P/B | **0.654** | 0.6 – 1.3 | Near floor |
| P/S | **0.141** | — | Very low |
| Net Margin | **−2.8%** | −2.8% to +3.9% | At trough |
| ROE | **−13%** | −13% to +17% | At trough |

- P/B has declined from **1.304** (Feb-2023) to **0.654** (Sep-2023), a **−49.8%** compression in nine months.
- The stock trades at **0.654× book** versus a 12-month high of **1.3×**, placing it at the lowest observed valuation in the supplied window.
- However, **P/B is low because ROE is negative (−13%)**, not because the market is mispricing stable earnings. The declining multiple is *consistent with* deteriorating fundamentals, not a disconnect from them.
- P/S of **0.141** reflects razor-thin margins (net margin **−2.8%**), not hidden value.
- PE is essentially meaningless with TTM net income of **−$3.73B**; the quoted 5.1–8.3 range likely uses forward estimates not supplied here.

### 4. Price Level & Return Context

- **Latest close:** $22.24 (Sep-29-2023), just **8.0%** above the 52-week low of **$20.58**, and **47.4%** below the 52-week high of **$42.29**.
- **Quarterly returns:** Q3-2023 **−21.9%**, Q2-2023 **−17.7%**, Q1-2023 **−7.8%**. Three consecutive quarters of accelerating decline.
- **Annual return:** **−33.0%** (from $33.20 on Sep-28-2022).
- The price decline *accelerated* each quarter, consistent with the fundamental deterioration in operating income and margins. The market's re-rating appears driven by worsening earnings power, not sentiment overshoot alone.

### 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside

**Upside arguments (quantitative only):**
- P/B at **0.654×** implies a **34.6% discount** to book value of ~$34.00/share. If normalized ROE returns toward the historical **+13–17%** range (last seen in early 2023), reversion to 1.0× book implies **$34.00**, a **+53%** return.
- Revenue growth is intact: **+8.6% YoY**, with TTM revenue of **$136.11B** at a record pace.
- The large impairment quarter (Nov-2022, EPS **−$4.31**) is likely non-recurring; excluding it, trailing EPS would be approximately **$1.36** ($0.81+$0.14−$0.48+$0.89), implying a normalized PE well below **20×**.

**Downside arguments (quantitative only):**
- Operating income has been negative in three of the last four quarters (−$415M, −$6,150M, −$320M, −$477M). TTM operating loss is **−$6.85B**. There is no evidence of stabilization.
- Gross margin is compressing QoQ despite revenue growth (**−6.6%** gross profit decline), suggesting structural cost issues.
- Equity fell **19.7%** in one quarter during the impairment event; a repeat or continued losses would erode book value, invalidating the P/B floor.
- Price is in a persistent downtrend with no support level holding — three straight accelerating quarterly losses (**−7.8% → −17.7% → −21.9%**).
- Cash generation is **unverifiable** (all n/a), removing the key metric that would support a value thesis.

### 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion

WBA trades at a deep discount to book (P/B **0.654**) and record-low margins, but the discount is *explained by* negative TTM operating income of **−$6.85B**, negative ROE of **−13%**, and accelerating margin erosion. Revenue growth (+8.6% YoY) provides a nominal floor, but the path from revenue to profit is broken: Q3 operating income is **−$477M** despite **$35.41B** in revenue.

The bull case depends entirely on earnings normalization — a return to historical operating margins (~2–3%) on a $136B+ revenue base would generate **$2.7–4.1B** in operating income and support the current book value. However, the supplied evidence shows *no quarter-over-quarter stabilization*; the trajectory is worsening.

Without cash-flow data, without a single positive operating-income trend quarter, and with the price decline accelerating in lockstep with fundamentals, the **quantitative evidence does not support a favorable risk/reward** at this point-in-time. The P/B discount is a *symptom*, not a signal of mispricing. Neutral-to-bearish on the numbers supplied.
WBA.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Analysis Report: Walgreens Boots Alliance (WBA)**
**Report Date:** Based on evidence through 2023-10-01

### 1. Business/Franchise Read
Based on the 10-K filed 2022-10-13, Walgreens Boots Alliance is a large-scale, integrated healthcare, pharmacy, and retail leader with approximately 13,000 locations globally (U.S., Europe, Latin America). Its operations are structured in three segments: U.S. Retail Pharmacy (core retail and pharmacy, including an equity investment in AmerisourceBergen), International (Boots-branded retail), and U.S. Healthcare (majority-owned assets VillageMD, Shields, and CareCentrix). The company is one of the world's largest purchasers of prescription drugs and positions itself as a critical part of the healthcare ecosystem, aiming to evolve into a provider of local clinical care services via value-based care models. A key franchise strength is its U.S. store density, with ~78% of the U.S. population living within five miles of a location. The business is exposed to reimbursement pressures from PBMs, insurers, and government programs.

### 2. Recent Developments (Dated)
*   **Leadership Change (2023-09-29):** Walgreens named Tim Wentworth, formerly of Express Scripts/Cigna, as its new CEO. This is noted as a potential long-term catalyst for profitability and a fresh perspective.
*   **Earnings Guidance Update (2023-09-29):** The company stated it expects FY23 adjusted earnings per share to be "at or near the low end" of its previously stated range.
*   **Strategic Partnerships (2023-09-12):** Walgreens partnered with Pearl Health to accelerate its push into value-based care, building on its recent investments in healthcare assets.
*   **Competitive Entry (2023-09-30):** Costco began offering members access to low-cost virtual primary care visits ($29) through a partnership with Sesame, representing a new competitive threat in healthcare services.
*   **Stock Performance (2023-09-28):** Multiple articles noted the stock had fallen to a share price not seen in over a decade and was among the worst-performing Dow stocks in 2023.

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks & Offsets
**Risks:**
*   **Legal and Regulatory:** Significant litigation risk, including an FDA warning and lawsuit over eye care products (2023-09-21), a lawsuit alleging Medicaid fraud (2023-08-15), and calls for boycotts related to dispensing decisions on mifepristone (2023-09-11). A settlement for $16M was also reported.
*   **Reimbursement and Regulatory Pressure:** Ongoing pressure from PBMs, Medicare, and Medicaid reimbursement cuts is a core risk highlighted in the 10-K. The Inflation Reduction Act adds complexity to drug pricing.
*   **Competitive Disruption:** New entrants like Costco offering low-cost healthcare services threaten traffic and market share.
*   **Execution Risk:** The strategy to transform into a healthcare provider through acquisitions (VillageMD, Shields, CareCentrix) requires significant integration and operational execution amid a challenging retail environment.

**Offsets:**
*   **New Leadership:** The appointment of a new CEO with relevant healthcare experience is viewed as a potential positive catalyst for strategic direction and profitability.
*   **Scale and Convenience:** The vast physical footprint and ongoing investment in an omni-channel platform (digital, delivery, drive-through) provide a durable competitive advantage in convenience.
*   **Healthcare Strategy Momentum:** The partnership with Pearl Health and the structuring of the U.S. Healthcare segment show active progress on the stated growth strategy.

### 4. Qualitative Assessment
**Neutral/Negative.** The confirmed evidence paints a mixed but concerning picture. The core pharmacy business faces severe, documented reimbursement headwinds, while the retail sector is grappling with theft-related closures and competitive threats. The recent downward earnings guidance and a stock price at decade lows reflect current operational and sentiment challenges. While the new CEO appointment and healthcare partnerships represent potential long-term positives, the immediate narrative is dominated by material legal risks, margin pressure, and the need to prove out a complex strategic transition. The balance of recent developments leans negative.

HON.quantitative_report:
### Quantitative Report: HON (Decision Date 2023-10-01)

#### 1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory
- **Revenue**: Quarterly revenue grew from $8.47B (Q3 2021) to $9.15B (Q2 2023), with YoY growth of +2.2% and QoQ growth of +3.2%. TTM revenue is $36.15B.
- **Earnings**: EPS diluted increased from $1.80 (Q3 2021) to $2.22 (Q2 2023), with YoY growth of +20.7% and QoQ growth of +7.2%. TTM net income is $5.48B.
- **Margins**: Gross profit YoY growth is +21.1%, indicating margin expansion. Valuation ratios report net margin of 0.152, gross margin of 0.357, and operating margin of 0.187 for the latest period.
- **Cash-Flow**: All cash-flow data is unavailable (supplied as "no data" for all quarters).
- **Balance-Sheet**: Total assets decreased from $64.47B (Q4 2021) to $62.34B (Q2 2023). Total equity decreased from $19.24B to $17.89B. Total debt increased from $16.06B to $18.55B.

#### 2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience
- **Earnings Quality**: Cash-flow data is unavailable, preventing assessment of cash conversion. Net income of $1.50B in Q2 2023 aligns with TTM net income of $5.48B, but without cash flow, earnings quality cannot be quantitatively verified.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience**: Debt-to-equity is 1.036, with total liabilities of $44.44B against total assets of $62.34B (liabilities-to-assets ratio ~71.3%). Equity has declined while debt increased, suggesting reduced resilience.

#### 3. Valuation versus the Company's Own Supplied History
- **Current Valuation**: As of 2023-09-29, PE diluted is 21.298, P/B is 6.521, P/S is 3.228, and earnings yield is 0.047.
- **Historical Context**: Over the past year (2022-10-03 to 2023-09-29), PE diluted ranged from 21.2 to 26.4, and P/B ranged from 6.1 to 7.7. Current PE is at the lower end of its range, while P/B is also near the lower end. Net margin and ROE have been stable around 0.152 and 0.306, respectively.

#### 4. Price Level and Quarterly/Annual Return Context versus the Fundamental Path
- **Price Level**: Latest close (2023-09-29) is $174.12, with a 52-week high of $208.26 and low of $157.05.
- **Returns**: Quarterly return (from 2023-06-30) is -11.0% (price dropped from $195.57). Annual return (from 2022-09-28) is +6.3% (price increased from $163.84). Historical quarterly returns show volatility: -10.9% in Q1 2023, +9.7% in Q2 2023, and +22.1% in Q4 2022.
- **Fundamental Comparison**: Recent price decline of 11.0% occurred alongside QoQ revenue growth of +3.2% and EPS growth of +7.2%, indicating a disconnect from near-term fundamentals.

#### 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside
- **Upside**: Valuation is at the low end of its historical range (PE 21.298 vs. range 21.2–26.4), with strong earnings growth (YoY EPS +20.7%). Margins are expanding (gross profit YoY +21.1%), and ROE is high at 0.306.
- **Downside**: Balance-sheet leverage is elevated (debt-to-equity 1.036, debt increased to $18.55B). Cash-flow data is absent, obscuring cash generation. Revenue growth is modest (YoY +2.2%), and price volatility is significant (quarterly moves up to ±22.1%).

#### 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion
Quantitatively, HON shows earnings growth and margin improvement, with valuation near historical lows. However, increased leverage and unknown cash flows pose risks, while price volatility may amplify downside. The risk/reward appears balanced: upside from valuation re-rating if earnings persist, tempered by balance-sheet and cash-flow uncertainties.
HON.qualitative_report:
### Qualitative Analysis Report: Honeywell International Inc. (HON)
**Decision Date:** 2023-10-01 | **Evidence Window:** 2023-08-12 to 2023-09-29

#### 1. Business/Franchise Read
Honeywell's strategic narrative, per its investor communications, focuses on simplifying its portfolio to align with three core megatrends: **Aviation, Automation, and Sustainability** (with an emphasis on Energy Transition), underpinned by digitalization. The company is actively targeting sustainable technology, renewable fuels, clean hydrogen, and carbon capture through its UOP strategy, noting "strong bookings" in these areas (2023-08-24). A specific growth vector is the **Connected Enterprise**, which Honeywell expects to drive "$750M+ of incremental growth" over the next three years across its segments (2023-09-28).

#### 2. Recent Developments (Dated)
- **2023-09-28:** Honeywell announced a **strategic collaboration with ESS Tech** to accelerate the adoption of long-duration energy storage (LDES), positioning itself within the growing renewable power grid transition.
- **2023-09-28:** The company confirmed its intention to **fund an asbestos trust with a one-time payment of $1.3 billion** to resolve its legal obligations (2023-09-28).
- **2023-09-05:** Honeywell agreed to **pay $160 million to settle Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) charges** with the SEC and DOJ related to conduct in Brazil and Algeria (2023-09-05).
- **2023-09-26/27:** Multiple **class-action lawsuits were filed** alleging Honeywell failed to protect personal information in a data breach (2023-09-26, 2023-09-27).

#### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
- **Risks:**
    - **Legal and Regulatory Liabilities:** The company faces material financial outflows from a $1.3 billion asbestos trust payment (2023-09-28) and a $160 million FCPA settlement (2023-09-05). Ongoing litigation regarding a data breach introduces reputational and potential financial risk (2023-09-26).
    - **Growth Outlook Concerns:** An analyst note expressed skepticism about Honeywell's near-term growth, citing a lack of expected catalysts to propel revenue growth beyond an estimated rate for the next three years (2023-09-20).
- **Offsets:**
    - **Segment Strength:** There is evidence of strong demand in the Aerospace segment due to commercial flight hour recovery and robust order books (2023-09-03).
    - **Strategic Positioning:** The company is actively investing in secular growth areas like energy transition (UOP strategy, ESS Tech collaboration) and digital enterprise solutions, which are intended to drive long-term incremental growth (2023-08-24, 2023-09-28).

#### 4. Qualitative Assessment
**Neutral.** The evidence presents a mixed picture. Honeywell is actively executing a strategy aligned with attractive, long-term megatrends (Energy Transition, Automation) and possesses strong segments like Aerospace. However, these positive strategic initiatives are counterbalanced by significant, confirmed near-term financial liabilities from legal settlements and ongoing litigation, alongside external commentary on a limited near-term growth catalyst. The net qualitative position is therefore balanced between strategic repositioning and material headwinds.

Balance-sheet deterioration and legal liabilities: Debt increased to $18.55B (debt-to-equity 1.036) while equity declined to $17.89B, compounded by a $1.3B asbestos trust payment and $160M FCPA settlement announced in September 2023.

ITEM 1.    BUSINESS
Overview
Salesforce is a global leader in customer relationship management ("CRM") technology that brings companies and their customers together. Founded in 1999, we enable companies of every size and industry to take advantage of powerful technologies to connect to their customers in a whole new way and help them transform their businesses around the customer in this digital-first world.
Our Customer 360 platform unites sales, service, marketing, commerce and IT teams by connecting customer data across systems, apps and devices to create a complete view of customers. With this single source of customer truth, teams can be more responsive, productive and efficient, deliver intelligent, personalized experiences across every channel and increase productivity. With Slack, we provide a digital headquarters where companies, employees, governments and stakeholders can collaborate to create success from anywhere.
Our Customer 360 service offerings are designed to be flexible, scalable and easy to use. They can generally be configured easily, deployed rapidly and integrated with other platforms and enterprise applications. We
…[truncated 20862 chars]…
consumer application companies that have entered the business software market; and
8
Table of Contents
- traditional platform development environment companies and cloud computing development platform companies who may develop toolsets and products that allow customers to build new apps that run on the customers' current infrastructure or as hosted services.
We believe more companies may become competitive threats due to the accelerated shift to cloud and hosted service offerings and customer experience management solutions. We also expect our competition to change and evolve as we expand into more markets, with new offerings.
Customers
We sell to businesses of all sizes and in almost every industry worldwide. The number of paying subscriptions at each of our customers ranges from one to hundreds of thousands. None of our customers accounted for more than five percent of our revenues in fiscal years 2023, 2022 or 2021. In addition, we do not have any material dependencies on any specific product, service or particular group or groups.
Customer Service and Support
We offer professional services to help customers achieve business results faster with Salesforce solutions. Our architects and innovation program teams act as advisors to plan and execute digital transformations for our customers. This includes implementation services for multi-cloud and complex deployments. We provide best-practices and AI-based recommendations and adoption programs globally. In addition, we provide advanced education, including in-person and online courses, to certify our customers and partners on architecting, administering, deploying and developing our service offerings.
Our global customer support group responds to both business and technical inquiries about the use of our products via the web, telephone, email, social networks and other channels. We provide standard customer support during regular business hours at no charge to customers who purchase any of our paying subscription editions. We also offer premier customer support that is either included in a premium success offering or sold for an additional fee, which can include services such as priority access to technical resources, developer support and system administration. In addition, we offer a premier priority support add-on that includes proactive monitoring, rapid incidence response and instruction from a dedicated support team knowledgeable about the customer's specific enterprise architecture.
Sales and Marketing
We sell our services primarily through our direct sales force, which comprises telephone sales personnel based in regional hubs, field sales personnel based in territories close to their customers and self-service offerings.
To a lesser extent, we also utilize a network of partners who refer sales leads to us and assist in selling to these prospects. This network includes global consulting firms, systems integrators and other partners. In return, we typically pay these partners a fee based on the first-year subscription revenue generated by the customers whom they refer. We continue to invest in developing additional distribution channels for our subscription services.
We use a variety of marketing programs across traditional and social channels to target our prospective and current customers, partners and developers. We focus our marketing activities in the cities and countries with the largest market opportunities. Our primary marketing activities include:
- multichannel marketing campaigns that span email, social media, the web, television and more, which align to a broader customer journey;
- in-person and virtual customer events of all sizes to create customer and prospect awareness, including proprietary events such as Dreamforce and our virtual Dreamforce to You, World Tours and other virtual events, as well as participation in trade shows and industry events;
- live events and original programming on our Salesforce+ streaming service, which includes discussions about the future of technology in the digital-first, work anywhere world and educational content to learn new skills and pursue new career opportunities;
- press and industry analyst relations to garner third-party validation and generate positive coverage for our company, brand, service offerings and value proposition;
- partner co-marketing activities with global and regional implementation partners;
- customer testimonials and our community of Trailblazers: individuals who drive innovation, grow their careers and transform their businesses using the Customer 360 platform;
- in-person and virtual technology event sponsorships; and
- event partnerships with high-profile global brands and organizations.
Intellectual Property
We rely on a combination of trademarks, copyrights, trade secrets and patents and contractual provisions, to protect our proprietary technology and our brands. We also enter into confidentiality and proprietary rights agreements with our employees, consultants and other third parties and control access to software, services, documentation and other proprietary information. We believe the duration of our patents is adequate relative to the expected lives of our service offerings. We also purchase or
9
Table of Contents
license technology that we incorporate into our products or services. At times, we make select intellectual property broadly available at no or low cost to achieve a strategic objective, such as promoting industry standards, advancing interoperability, supporting open source software or attracting and enabling our external development community. While it may be necessary in the future to seek or renew licenses relating to various aspects of our products and business methods, we believe, based upon past experience and industry practice, such licenses generally could be obtained on commercially reasonable terms.
Human Capital Management
Salesforce is committed to a core set of values: trust, customer success, innovation, equality and sustainability. These core values are the foundation of our company culture, which we believe is fundamental to, and a competitive advantage in, our approach to managing our workforce. We believe our company culture fosters open dialogue, collaboration, recognition and a sense of family, all of which allow us to attract and retain the best talent, which is critical for our continued success. For example, our sales, engineering and customer success teams are critical to our ability to grow, innovate and ensure the trust and customer success of our customers.
We believe our efforts in managing our workforce have been effective. Our focus on our workplace environment and a strong company culture has led to recognition across the globe, as evidenced by the following awards: Fortune World's Most Admired Companies (2022 and for the eighth year in a row), Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For (2022 and for the 13th year in a row), Human Rights Campaign Best Places to Work for LGBTQ Equality (2022) and Glassdoor Employees' Choice Best Place to Work in Canada, France, Germany, the United Kingdom and the United States (2022).
As of January 31, 2023, we had 79,390 employees, of which approximately 52 percent were located in the United States and 48 percent were located internationally and approximately 36 percent identified as women, 63 percent identified as men and less than 0.2 percent identified as non-binary. This number includes those individuals impacted by our restructuring plan announced on January 4, 2023, which includes a reduction of our current workforce by approximately 10 percent. None of our employees in the United States are represented by a labor union. However, employees of certain foreign subsidiaries are represented by works councils.
We have continued to invest in equality, diversity and inclusion initiatives, development programs, employee engagement and ongoing communications and feedback. Some of our key human

CRM.quantitative_report:
### **Quantitative Assessment: CRM (Salesforce, Inc.)**
**Decision Date:** 2023-10-01 | **Point-in-Time Evidence:** Through 2023-09-29

---

#### **1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**
*   **Revenue Trajectory:** Demonstrated consistent growth. Latest Q2 (2023-07-31) revenue was **$8.60B**, up **+4.3%** QoQ and **+11.4%** YoY. The quarterly series shows acceleration from **$6.86B** in Q3 2021.
*   **Earnings Trajectory:** Highly volatile. Latest Q2 2023 EPS diluted was **$1.28**, a sharp recovery from **$0.20** in Q1 2023 and **-$0.10** in Q4 2022. YoY EPS growth is **+1728.6%**, reflecting a low base from prior-period losses or small profits.
*   **Margin Trajectory:** Gross margin is strong and stable at approximately **75.5%** (e.g., **$6.49B** / **$8.60B**). Operating margin expanded to **17.2%** in Q2 2023 (**$1.48B** / **$8.60B**) from near-zero or negative levels in prior quarters. TTM net margin was **4.8%**.
*   **Cash-Flow Trajectory:** **Data is unavailable** for the entire 8-quarter lookback period. Free cash flow and operating cash flow cannot be assessed.
*   **Balance-Sheet Trajectory:** The balance sheet is large and relatively stable. Total assets were **$92.45B**, total equity **$58.08B**, and total debt **$9.42B** as of Q2 2023. Debt has decreased from **$10.60B** in prior periods.

#### **2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** The absence of cash flow data is a critical gap, preventing assessment of earnings-to-cash conversion. Reported net income shows significant volatility (**-$0.10** to **$1.28** EPS over the last four quarters), which may indicate sensitivity to operating leverage or non-cash items. The recent high YoY growth (**+1763.2%** net income) is from an exceptionally low base.
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** Appears resilient based on leverage metrics. Debt-to-equity is low at **0.162**. Total equity has remained in a relatively tight band around **$57-60B** over eight quarters, indicating retained earnings stability despite quarterly income volatility.

#### **3. Valuation versus History**
*   **Current Valuation (2023-09-29):** PE (diluted) = **126.7**, Price/Book = **3.44**, Price/Sales = **6.05**, Earnings Yield = **0.8%**.
*   **Historical Context (2022-10-03 to 2023-09-29):** The PE ratio has compressed significantly from its recent range. It reached **1,063.7** in the window and was **551.9** as recently as 2023-08-28. The current PE of **126.7** is the lowest in the provided 12-point history and sits at the absolute floor of the 365-day range (**126.5-1063.7**). Net margin and ROE have improved to recent highs (**0.048** and **0.027**, respectively), supporting the PE compression.

#### **4. Price Level and Return Context**
*   **Price Level:** The stock closed at **$202.78** on 2023-09-29. This is below the ~52-week high of **$238.22** and above the low of **$126.34**.
*   **Return Context:** The stock has a **+35.0%** return over the past year (since 2022-09-28). However, the most recent quarter showed a decline of **-4.0%** (since 2023-06-30). The price path from late 2022 shows a massive rally: **+53.0%** from 2022-12-28 to 2023-03-30.

#### **5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**
*   **Upside:** The combination of consistent revenue growth (**+11.4%** YoY) and sharply expanding operating margins (from ~0% to **17.2%**) creates a powerful earnings growth dynamic. The current PE valuation is at a historic low relative to its own recent history, potentially reflecting improved earnings durability not yet fully priced.
*   **Downside:** The complete absence of cash flow data obscures the quality of reported earnings. The PE of **126.7** remains exceptionally high in absolute terms, leaving the stock vulnerable to any disappointment in the pace of margin expansion or revenue growth. The recent quarterly price decline of **-4.0%** precedes this analysis.

#### **6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The fundamental path shows strengthening revenue and a decisive inflection in profitability margins. The valuation has compressed to a historic low on a relative basis (PE **126.7** vs. recent >**500**). However, this valuation remains high absolutely and is predicated on the continuation of recent margin expansion, the sustainability of which cannot be fully validated due to the lack of cash flow data and historical earnings volatility. The risk/reward is framed by improving fundamentals versus a still-premium valuation with incomplete evidence on cash generation.
CRM.qualitative_report:
### **Qualitative Assessment Report: Salesforce (CRM)**  
**Decision Date:** 2023-10-01  

#### **1. Business/Franchise Read**  
From the 2023-03-08 10-K filing, Salesforce is positioned as a global leader in CRM technology. Its **Customer 360 platform** integrates sales, service, marketing, commerce, IT, and collaboration tools (including Slack) to provide a unified view of customer data. The business model is primarily **subscription-based**, sold directly and through partners to companies of all sizes globally. Key competitive advantages cited include scalability, a broad integrated suite, a strong partner ecosystem, and a focus on industry-specific solutions (e.g., financial services, healthcare). The company emphasizes its core values—trust, innovation, and equality—as foundational to its culture and strategy. Growth strategies focus on cross-selling/up-selling to existing customers, geographic expansion, and continuous product innovation (e.g., Genie Customer Data Cloud). The competitive landscape is noted as "highly competitive, rapidly evolving and fragmented" with low barriers to entry.

#### **2. Recent Developments (Point-in-Time)**  
*   **Financial Guidance & Profitability (2023-08-31):** Salesforce raised its annual adjusted operating margin forecast to approximately 30% from 28%, with the CFO stating this is "a floor, not a ceiling." This followed a strong earnings report and resulted in a stock surge.  
*   **Cost Management & Activist Response (2023-08-30):** The company was noted to have executed layoffs, cost cuts, and a price increase, which helped address earlier concerns from activist investors about growth and margins.  
*   **Strategic Acquisition (2023-09-22):** Salesforce announced an agreement to buy Airkit.ai, an AI customer service startup, to enhance its Service Cloud with conversational AI capabilities.  
*   **Market Sentiment (2023-09-29):** The stock was reported to have declined 8.3% in the 30 days following its last earnings report (late August).  
*   **Broader Industry Context (2023-09-29):** A report highlighted over 238,000 global tech layoffs year-to-date in 2023, indicating a significant industry-wide cost-cutting environment.  

#### **3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**  
**Risks:**  
*   **Legal & Regulatory Exposure (2023-08-12, 2023-08-14, 2023-08-25):** Evidence includes a lawsuit from a former executive alleging wrongful termination after raising concerns about misleading claims regarding the real-time capabilities of its Customer Data Platform (CDP). Additionally, there are references to a data breach and consumer litigation under California privacy laws (CCPA), posing reputational and financial risk.  
*   **Competitive Pressure (2023-09-25):** The market frames an active competitive debate between Salesforce and other cloud CRM providers like HubSpot, which has shown strong market performance.  
*   **Execution Risk on AI/Integration (2023-09-22):** The acquisition of Airkit.ai represents a step to bolster AI capabilities, but successful integration and execution in a fast-moving AI field are not guaranteed.  

**Offsets:**  
*   **Strengthened Financial Narrative (2023-08-31, 2023-08-30):** The raised margin guidance and demonstrated cost discipline provide a clear offset to prior growth/margin skepticism, indicating improved operational efficiency.  
*   **Proactive Portfolio Management (2023-09-22):** The targeted acquisition of AI talent and technology (Airkit.ai) directly addresses the strategic priority of embedding advanced AI across its platform.  
*   **Diversified Customer Base (10-K, 2023-03-08):** No single customer accounts for more than 5% of revenue, reducing dependency risk.  

#### **4. Qualitative Assessment**  
**Neutral with a Positive Tilt.** The supplied evidence presents a balanced picture. The positive narrative is supported by recent, confirmed **improvements in financial guidance and margin discipline** (August 2023), alongside strategic actions like the **Airkit.ai acquisition** to strengthen its AI offering. However, these are offset by clear **near-term headwinds**, including a descending stock trajectory post-earnings, a challenging macro environment with widespread tech layoffs, and tangible **legal risks** from allegations of product misrepresentation and data privacy breaches. The franchise itself, as described in the 10-K, remains robust and broadly integrated, but execution and legal/regulatory vigilance are critical. The qualitative evidence leans slightly positive due to the concrete operational and strategic moves made in August-September 2023, but legal and competitive risks temper an outright bullish view.

Item 1. Business
The Boeing Company, together with its subsidiaries (herein referred to as "Boeing," the "Company," "we," "us," "our"), is one of the world's major aerospace firms.
We are organized based on the products and services we offer. We operate in four reportable segments:
- 
Commercial Airplanes (BCA);
- 
Defense, Space & Security (BDS);
- 
Global Services (BGS);
- 
Boeing Capital (BCC).
Commercial Airplanes Segment
This segment develops, produces and markets commercial jet aircraft principally to the commercial airline industry worldwide. We are a leading producer of commercial aircraft and offer a family of commercial jetliners designed to meet a broad spectrum of global passenger and cargo requirements of airlines. This family of commercial jet aircraft in production includes the 737 narrow-body model and the 767, 777 and 787 wide-body models. We ended production of the 747 wide-body model in 2022. Development continues on the 777X program and the 737-7 and 737-10 derivatives.
Defense, Space & Security Segment
This segment engages in the research, development, production and modification of manned and unmanned military aircraft and weapons systems for strike, surveillance and mobility, including fighter and trainer aircraft; vertical lift, including rotorcraft and tilt-rotor aircraft; and commercial derivative aircraft, including anti-submarine and tanker aircraft. In addition, this segment engages in the research, development, production and modification of the following products and related services: strategic defense and intelligence systems, including strategic missile and defense systems, command, control, communications, computers, intelligence, surveillance and reconnaissance (C4ISR), cyber and information solutions, and intelligence systems, satellite systems, including government and commercial satellites and space exploration.
Global Services Segment
This segment provides services to our commercial and defense customers worldwide. BGS sustains aerospace platforms and systems with a full spectrum of products and services, including supply chain and logistics management, engineering, maintenance and modifications, upgrades and conversions, spare parts, pilot and maintenance training systems and services, technical and maintenance documents, and data analytics and digital services.
Boeing Capital Segment
BCC seeks to ensure that Boeing customers have the financing they need to buy and take delivery of their Boeing product, while managing overall financing exposure. BCC's portfolio consists of equipment under operating leases, sales-type/finance leases, notes and other receivables, assets held for sale or re-lease and investments.

Intellectual Property
We own numerous patents and
…[truncated 7569 chars]…
ion (FAA) and the Department of Homeland Security. Similar government authorities exist in our non-U.S. markets.
Government Contracts.
The U.S. government, and other governments, may terminate any of our government contracts at their convenience, as well as for default based on our failure to meet specified performance requirements. If any of our U.S. government contracts were to be terminated for convenience, we generally would be entitled to receive payment for work completed and allowable termination or cancellation costs. If any of our government contracts were to be terminated for default, generally the U.S. government would pay only for the work that has been accepted and could require us to pay the difference between the original contract price and the cost to re-procure the contract items, net of the work accepted from the original contract. The U.S. government can also hold us liable for damages resulting from the default.
Commercial Aircraft.
In the U.S., our commercial aircraft products are required to comply with FAA regulations governing production and quality systems, airworthiness and installation approvals, repair procedures and continuing operational safety. New aircraft models and new derivative aircraft are required to obtain FAA certification prior to entry into service. Outside the U.S., similar requirements exist for airworthiness, installation and operational approvals. These requirements are generally administered by the national aviation authorities of each country and, in the case of Europe, coordinated by the European Union Aviation Safety Agency.
Environmental.
We are subject to various federal, state, local and non-U.S. laws and regulations relating to environmental protection, including the discharge, treatment, storage, disposal and remediation of hazardous substances and wastes. We could also be affected by laws and regulations relating to climate change, including laws limiting or otherwise related to greenhouse gas emissions. These laws and regulations could lead to increased environmental compliance expenditures, increased energy and raw materials costs and new and/or additional investment in designs and technologies. We continually assess our compliance status and management of environmental matters to ensure our operations are in compliance with all applicable environmental laws and regulations. Investigation, remediation and operation and maintenance costs associated with environmental compliance and management of sites are a normal, recurring part of our operations. These costs often are allowable costs under our contracts with the U.S. government. It is reasonably possible that costs incurred to ensure continued environmental compliance could have a material impact on our results of operations, financial condition or cash flows if additional work requirements or more stringent clean-up standards are imposed by regulators, new areas of soil, air and groundwater contamination are discovered and/or expansions of work scope are prompted by the results of investigations.
For additional information relating to environmental contingencies, see Note 13 to our Consolidated Financial Statements.
Non-U.S. Sales.
Our non-U.S. sales are subject to both U.S. and non-U.S. governmental regulations and procurement policies and practices, including regulations relating to import-export control, tariffs, investment, exchange controls, anti-corruption and repatriation of earnings. Non-U.S. sales are also subject to varying currency, political and economic risks.
Raw Materials, Parts and Subassemblies
We are highly dependent on the availability of essential materials, parts and subassemblies from our suppliers and subcontractors. The most important raw materials required for our aerospace products
4
Table of Contents
are aluminum (sheet, plate, forgings and extrusions), titanium (sheet, plate, forgings and extrusions) and composites (including carbon and boron). Although alternative sources generally exist for these raw materials, qualification of the sources could take a year or more. During 2022, as a result of the Russia Ukraine war, we suspended purchasing titanium from Russia. This has not disrupted our operations as we have been able to use inventory on hand and identify alternative sources. Many major components and product equipment items are procured or subcontracted on a sole-source basis. We continue to work with a small number of sole-source suppliers to ensure continuity of supply for certain items.
Suppliers
We are dependent upon the ability of a large number of U.S. and non-U.S. suppliers and subcontractors to meet performance specifications, quality standards and delivery schedules at our anticipated costs. While we maintain an extensive qualification and performance surveillance system to control risk associated with such reliance on third parties, failure of suppliers or subcontractors to meet commitments could adversely affect production schedules and program/contract profitability, thereby jeopardizing our ability to fulfill commitments to our customers. We are also dependent on the availability of energy sources, such as electricity, at affordable prices.
Seasonality
No material portion of our business is considered to be seasonal.
Executive Officers of the Registrant
See "Item 10. Directors, Executive Officers and Corporate Governance" in Part III.
Other Information
Boeing was originally incorporated in the State of Washington in 1916 and reincorporated in Delaware in 1934. Our principal executive offices are located at 929 Long Bridge Drive, Arlington, Virginia 22202, and our telephone number is (703) 465-3500.
General information about us can be found at www.boeing.com. The information contained on or connected to our website is not incorporated by reference into this Annual Report on Form 10-K and should not be considered part of this or any other report filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). Our Annual Report on Form 10-K, Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q and Current Reports on Form 8-K, as well as any amendments to those reports, are available free of charge through our website as soon as reasonably practicable after we file them with, or furnish them to, the SEC. The SEC maintains a website at www.sec.gov that contains reports, proxy statements and other information regarding SEC registrants, including Boeing.
Forward-Looking Statements
This report, as well as our annual report to shareholders, quarterly reports and other filings we make with the SEC, press and earnings releases and other written and oral communications, contain "forward-looking statements" within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. Words such as "may," "should," "expects," "intends," "projects," "plans," "believes," "estimates," "targets," "anticipates" and similar expressions generally identify these forward-looking statements.
